2604.26998.title: Entropy-Dominated Temporal Vocal Dynamics as Digital Biomarkers for Depression Detection
2604.26998.paper_summary: Automated depression detection often relies on static aggregation of conversational signals, potentially obscuring clinically meaningful behavioral dynamics. We investigated whether entropy-driven temporal biomarkers improve depression detection beyond standard pooled features using the DAIC-WOZ corpus. Using 142 labeled participants, we reconstructed utterance-level acoustic trajectories and compared pooled temporal baselines, trajectory dynamics, Shannon entropy biomarkers, recurrence quantification, sample entropy, fractal complexity, and coupling biomarkers under leakage-aware validation. Static pooling achieved an AUC of 0.593, trajectory dynamics improved performance to 0.637, and entropy biomarkers produced the strongest statistically significant improvement over pooled baselines (AUC 0.646; nested cross-validated AUC 0.615; permutation p = 0.017). Entropy biomarkers outperformed recurrence, coupling, sample entropy, and fractalbased features, with several biomarkers stable across folds. These findings suggest depression-related signal may lie less in average acoustic levels than in entropy of conversational dynamics, supporting temporally informed digital phenotypes for mental-health assessment.
2604.26998.paper_summary_zh: 自動化的憂鬱症檢測通常依賴於對對話信號的靜態聚合，這可能會掩蓋臨床上有意義的行為動態。我們調查了基於熵驅動的時間生物標記是否能在超越標準聚合特徵的情況下改善憂鬱症檢測，使用了DAIC-WOZ語料庫。使用142名標記參與者，我們重建了發話級別的聲學軌跡，並比較了聚合的時間基線、軌跡動態、香農熵生物標記、重複量化、樣本熵、分形複雜度和耦合生物標記，並在考慮洩漏的驗證下進行了評估。靜態聚合達到了0.593的AUC，軌跡動態將性能提升至0.637，而熵生物標記則在聚合基線上產生了最強的統計顯著性改善（AUC 0.646；嵌套交叉驗證AUC 0.615；置換p = 0.017）。熵生物標記的表現超過了重複、耦合、樣本熵和基於分形的特徵，其中幾個生物標記在不同的摺疊中保持穩定。這些發現表明，與憂鬱症相關的信號可能不在於平均聲學水平，而在於對話動態的熵，支持針對心理健康評估的時間信息數字表型。
2604.26998.author: Himadri S Samanta et.al.
